briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — finesse winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-p
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, flat, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 3XD for the deep zone in cold water. Smaller profile, tight action, gets 12-15ft on 10lb fluoro. Fish steeper rocky banks, deeper transition zones. Make long casts to keep the bait in the zone fo
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, flat, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Grassy lakes have 3 depth zones: Shallow flats (0-4ft) — topwater, spinnerbaits, weedless plastics for sight-feeding fish. Weed edges (4-8ft) — the sweet spot, bass ambush from the edge, use soft plastics, jigs, creature baits. Deeper transi...

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

Wheeler: Big fish spots are big giant flats in 22-29 ft with tiny isolated rockpiles — a boulder in the middle of a rocky flat, or a hard-bottom spot. Only good for 1-2 fish and time-consuming to find, but that's what separates you

Hackney: 'For winter deep-water jig fishing on lowland reservoirs with stumps and channel swings in 6-8 feet, I use 16-20 pound fluorocarbon. On hard structure like rock I go 14-16 pound. Only downsize to 12 in extremely deep clear water'

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
